name: Magahi
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Magahi is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Magahi (मगही) is an Eastern Indo-Aryan (Bihari) language of Bihar and Jharkhand, India, written in DEVANAGARI — an ABUGIDA: each consonant letter carries an inherent vowel /ə/ which is cancelled by virama and overridden by vowel matras, and the mapping must also handle schwa deletion. INPUT CONTRACT: Devanagari Magahi text (Unicode, NFC). PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode maga1260), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode maga1260.

ancestors.notes: Indo-Aryan — the classification node this language sits under.
